sample.scope: Task 3 verifyLedger(path) vs spec 'validators reject locators whose repository_ref does not match the derived value'
sample.claim: The spec requires validators to reject locators whose repository_ref differs from the derived value. verifyLedger(path) is the consumer-facing validator and only checks agreement with the header's repository_ref and shape—it has no repoRoot/git accessor and cannot re-derive. Even an honest driver that derived from the wrong cwd publishes a ledger that verifies ok:true; the spec-mandated derived-value check is not implementable in the shipped verifier signature and exists only as a Task 6 test-side assertion.

verdict.reason: Error: Anthropic API error 400: {"type":"error","error":{"type":"invalid_request_error","message":"Your credit balance is too low to access the Anthropic API. P

sample.scope: D16/D18 shallow guard vs partial clones (--filter=blob:none)
sample.claim: The shallow-history guard checks is-shallow-repository only. A partial clone can report is-shallow-repository=false yet lack historical blobs; git log -S may lazily fetch objects (network, subprocess, object-db writes) during a supposedly read-only weave—violating read-only and isolation assumptions without tripping the stable shallow error.

verdict.reason: Error: Anthropic API error 400: {"type":"error","error":{"type":"invalid_request_error","message":"Your credit balance is too low to access the Anthropic API. P
